frames.name: Drug Pricing Policy Implementation
frames.description: Focuses on Trump's 'most-favored nation' drug pricing policy as a concrete policy mechanism with measurable impacts, particularly on international pricing dynamics
frames.emphasis: Policy effectiveness and real-world implementation of drug pricing reforms

frames.name: Political Strategy and Messaging
frames.description: Frames the administration's health policies through the lens of political positioning and attempts to control narrative focus
frames.emphasis: Trump administration's efforts to shift attention from controversial vaccine policies to popular issues like drug pricing

frames.name: Medicaid Program Expansion
frames.description: Presents the story as healthcare program development with focus on expanding pilot programs to include more pharmaceutical companies
frames.emphasis: Operational aspects of expanding Medicaid 'most-favored nations' pilot program

outlierPerspectives: Vaccine expert criticism framing anti-vaccine government positions as 'stupid' and 'immoral'
International pricing implications suggesting higher drug prices abroad as consequence of US policy
missingContext: Specific details of how 'most-favored nation' pricing mechanism works
Quantitative data on current vaccination rate declines mentioned
Details about which pharmaceutical companies are participating or being recruited for Medicaid pilot
Timeline and implementation details of the drug pricing policies
Comparison with previous administrations' drug pricing approaches
Industry response to these policy changes
